name: Jugged Hare
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the interior of the hare, carefully preserving the thick blood.
2. Skin the hare, wipe it clean, and cut into joints. Wash the head, heart, and liver in cold salted water, then add them with the blood and bones to a saucepan.
3. Add 4 pints of cold water and a teaspoon of salt. Bring to a boil, skim off any scum, and add the herbs tied in muslin, along with pepper and mace.
4. Peel the carrot and wash the onion. Place cloves into the vegetables and add them to the stock. Simmer all ingredients for 3–4 hours, skimming frequently to remove grease.
5. Dust the hare joints with flour, melt butter or shortening in a pan, and fry until lightly browned. Transfer to a large casserole. Mix equal parts flour and water into a smooth paste. When the stock is ready, strain it through a fine mesh into another saucepan and add the thickener.
6. Stir well and bring to a boil for a few minutes. If needed, add a few drops of browning.
7. Pour the strained gravy over the fried hare so that it is just covered. Place in a moderately hot oven and cook for about 2 hours. Just before serving, stir in the port wine.
8. Serve by transferring the hare to a large dish with some gravy poured around. The remaining gravy can be served separately, accompanied by red current jelly.
